title: Detached HEAD work must be anchored to a branch before normal push
summary: Agents may make commits while Git is in detached HEAD state, then fail or loop when `git push` cannot infer a branch. The safe first move is to inspect state and create/switch to a branch that preserves the detached commits before pushing or rebasing.
ai_value: Prevents agents from losing work or applying destructive Git commands when a commit exists outside a named branch. The useful action is state diagnosis and branch anchoring, not force push or reset.

contexts: repository is in detached HEAD state
agent has local commits or changes it wants to preserve
normal git push fails because no current branch exists

trigger_signals.description: Short branch status shows HEAD with no branch.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Do not assume the agent is on main/master/current feature branch; detached commits may not be reachable by a branch.

trigger_signals.description: Plain git push fails because there is no current branch.
trigger_signals.pattern: fatal: You are not currently on a branch\.|To push the history leading to the current \(detached HEAD\) state now
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: The next safe action is to preserve the current HEAD on a named branch, not to retry push blindly.

trigger_signals.description: git branch --show-current prints an empty line.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Empty current branch output is consistent with detached HEAD; confirm with status before branch operations.

negative_signals.description: git branch --show-current prints a non-empty branch name.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: If Git is already on a branch, this detached-HEAD recovery trap may not apply; handle upstream/push configuration separately.

negative_signals.description: The repository has no commits yet or is on an unborn branch.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: Unborn branch setup is a different Git initialization case, not detached HEAD recovery.

common_wrong_assumptions: The current commit must already belong to main or the last visible branch.
Retrying git push with different remote names will fix detached HEAD.
A force push, hard reset, or checkout is a safe first response.
Detached HEAD means the work is lost.

first_checks.check: Inspect the branch/detached state without modifying files.
first_checks.why: Agents must know whether HEAD is attached before choosing a push or branch operation.
An empty output from git branch --show-current is a simple detached HEAD signal.

first_checks.check: Show recent commits and refs that may need preservation.
first_checks.why: Before creating/switching branches, identify whether current HEAD contains work not on an existing branch.

decision_rules.condition: Git status shows detached HEAD and the current HEAD contains work that should be kept.
decision_rules.then: Create a new branch at the current commit with `git switch -c <safe-branch-name>` before pushing or opening a PR. Choose a descriptive branch name; do not discard changes.

decision_rules.condition: Git is detached only for temporary inspection and there is no work to keep.
decision_rules.then: Switch back to the intended branch with `git switch <branch>` or `git switch -`; avoid carrying unreviewed detached-state assumptions into later commands.

decision_rules.condition: There are uncommitted changes and switching/branching may conflict.
decision_rules.then: Stop before using stash, reset, checkout, or discard-style options unless the owner explicitly approves the data-loss risk.

do_not: Do not run `git reset --hard`, `git switch --discard-changes`, or equivalent destructive commands as the first response.
Do not force push from detached HEAD to an existing protected branch.
Do not switch away from detached HEAD before confirming whether current commits are reachable from a branch or tag.
Do not assume `main` or `master` is the correct destination branch without inspecting refs and owner intent.

agent_directives.preferred_next_step: Run safe read-only Git state checks, then preserve detached work by creating a named branch if the work should be kept.

risk.notes: Branch creation is usually safe, but reset/discard/force-push operations can lose work or rewrite shared history and require a gate.

sources.excerpt: Git status porcelain v2 branch headers include branch.head, which can be a branch or detached.
sources.supports: Official git-status documentation describes branch headers that identify current branch or detached state.

sources.excerpt: git switch changes branches and updates working tree/index to match the branch.
sources.supports: Official git-switch documentation describes switching to branches.

sources.excerpt: git switch -c creates a new branch at a start point and switches to it.
sources.supports: Official git-switch documentation describes creating and switching to a new branch.

sources.excerpt: git switch --detach switches to a commit for inspection or discardable experiments; if work is worth keeping, create a new name for it.
sources.supports: Official git-switch documentation describes detached HEAD and creating a branch to keep work.

sources.excerpt: git switch - can switch back to the previous branch.
sources.supports: Official git-switch documentation describes switching back to the previous branch.

sources.excerpt: git switch refuses operations that would lose local changes unless told otherwise with discard/merge options.
sources.supports: Official git-switch documentation describes local-change preservation and discard options.

sources.excerpt: Local reproduction showed `## HEAD (no branch)` and `fatal: You are not currently on a branch` after committing in detached HEAD state and running plain git push.
sources.supports: Local verification confirms the trigger signals and push failure in a detached HEAD state with a configured remote.

verification_runs.command: Create a temporary git repo and bare remote, commit on main, switch --detach to an earlier commit, commit detached work, then run git status --short --branch, git branch --show-current, and git push.
verification_runs.observed_result: git status --short --branch printed `## HEAD (no branch)`; git branch --show-current printed an empty line; plain git push printed `fatal: You are not currently on a branch` with advice to push HEAD to a named remote branch.
verification_runs.conclusion: Detached HEAD work is not on a normal current branch, so an agent should preserve it with a named branch before ordinary push/PR workflow.
